name: Honeycomb MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Honeycomb MCP server enabling AI agents to query Honeycomb's observability platform — running queries, exploring datasets, analyzing traces, and investigating performance issues with Honeycomb's powerful query engine.
use_cases: Running Honeycomb queries for performance investigation from agents
Analyzing trace data for latency and error root cause analysis
Querying high-cardinality observability data for incident response
Exploring Honeycomb datasets for automated performance baseline tracking
On-call incident investigation using Honeycomb's trace analysis
not_for: Teams not using Honeycomb for observability
Simple metric alerting (use Prometheus for that)
best_when: An agent needs to investigate distributed traces, query high-cardinality observability data, or perform root cause analysis in a Honeycomb-instrumented system.
avoid_when: You're using Datadog, Lightstep, or another observability platform — use their native integrations.

auth.notes: API keys with team-level and dataset-level access. Configuration keys vs query keys (ingest vs query). Query keys used for agent read access.

pricing.notes: Generous free tier for observability. Honeycomb is premium in the observability space but highly capable.

performance.rate_limits: Query API: rate limits apply by plan; not prominently documented

known_agent_gotchas: Honeycomb queries can be slow for large time ranges — use appropriate time bounds
Dataset must be specified for all queries — agents must know which dataset to target
Query syntax is specific to Honeycomb — not Prometheus or SQL
High-cardinality queries are Honeycomb's strength but require knowing the field names
Ingest key vs query key — use query key for read-only agent access
Results include statistical summaries that require understanding Honeycomb's calculation model
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Query keys vs ingest keys provide some granularity. Team and dataset scoping available. SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Query vs ingest key separation is good security design. Team and dataset scoping. SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001.
